prevalence.source: Academia Brasileira de Neurologia / epidemiological syntheses and IHME GBD estimates
prevalence.trend: increasing due to population aging and improved survival with chronic diseases

incidence.source: Epidemiological cohort syntheses (Latin America) / IHME GBD

burden.mortalityRate: Substantial in older age groups (direct and indirect)

vulnerablePopulations: rural populations with limited geriatric/neurology access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with functional impairment (DSM-5/ICD criteria), cognitive testing (e.g., MMSE/MoCA), and workup for reversible causes; neuroimaging when available

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited opportunistic screening in APS; cognitive testing use varies
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Symptom management variable; caregiver support and access to multidisciplinary services limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care recognition variable; specialist confirmation (neurology/geriatrics) concentrated in larger cities
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Non-pharmacologic support (caregiver education, safety, cognitive stimulation); cholinesterase inhibitors and memantine when indicated; management of vascular risk factors
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, limited specialist and long-term care capacity, and heavy caregiver burden with inequitable support services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing awareness and availability of core dementia medications; expanding APS capacity for chronic disease management
